Package coxprogramming.processing.sprites

Class Sprite

Object
Sprite
Direct Known Subclasses:
Blob, CompoundSprite, ImageSprite
public abstract class Sprite extends Object
The base class for every sprite in the game.

A sprite is any object that lives on the screen, has a position, and knows how to draw itself. Sprite is abstract, meaning you cannot create a Sprite directly â€” instead you create a subclass like Blob or Block that fills in the drawSprite() method with the actual drawing code.

Every sprite stores its position and velocity as Vector2D objects. What happens when a sprite reaches the edge of the screen is controlled by the static screenBehavior field â€” change it once in setup() and every sprite obeys it automatically.

Pass your sketch's this reference as the first argument to any constructor so the sprite can access screen dimensions and draw itself: 

   Blob b = new Blob(this, 100, 200, 30, color(255, 0, 0));

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Sprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y)
    Creates a sprite at position (x, y) with no initial velocity.
    Sprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y, float dx, float dy)
    Creates a sprite at position (x, y) with a given starting velocity.
    Sprite(PApplet parent, Vector2D s)
    Creates a sprite at the position given by a Vector2D, with no initial velocity.

  • Field Details

    • parent

      protected PApplet parent
      A reference to the Processing sketch that owns this sprite. Used to call drawing functions and read screen size, mouse position, etc.

    • pos

      protected Vector2D pos
      The sprite's current position on the screen.

    • vel

      protected Vector2D vel
      The sprite's current velocity â€” how far it moves each frame.

    • screenBehavior

      public static ScreenBehavior screenBehavior
      Controls what happens to every sprite when it reaches the edge of the screen. Change this once in your sketch's setup() and all sprites obey it.
      • ScreenBehavior.Wrapping â€” sprite exits one edge and re-enters the opposite (default)
      • ScreenBehavior.HardWalls â€” sprite stops at the edge and velocity on that axis is zeroed
      • ScreenBehavior.BouncyWalls â€” sprite bounces by reversing the relevant velocity component
      • ScreenBehavior.NoWalls â€” sprite moves freely and can travel off-screen

      Example:

         void setup() {
     Sprite.screenBehavior = ScreenBehavior.BouncyWalls;
   }

  • Constructor Details

    • Sprite

      public Sprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y)
      Creates a sprite at position (x, y) with no initial velocity.
      Parameters:
      parent - your Processing sketch â€” pass this
      x - the starting x position (pixels from the left edge)
      y - the starting y position (pixels from the top edge)

    • Sprite

      public Sprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y, float dx, float dy)
      Creates a sprite at position (x, y) with a given starting velocity.
      Parameters:
      parent - your Processing sketch â€” pass this
      x - the starting x position
      y - the starting y position
      dx - horizontal velocity in pixels per frame (positive = right)
      dy - vertical velocity in pixels per frame (positive = down)

    • Sprite

      public Sprite(PApplet parent, Vector2D s)
      Creates a sprite at the position given by a Vector2D, with no initial velocity.
      Parameters:
      parent - your Processing sketch â€” pass this
      s - the starting position as a Vector2D

  • Method Details

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(Vector2D newPosition)
      Instantly moves the sprite to the position described by newPosition.
      Parameters:
      newPosition - the new position as a Vector2D

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(float x, float y)
      Instantly moves the sprite to the coordinates (x, y).
      Parameters:
      x - the new x position
      y - the new y position

    • setVelocity

      public void setVelocity(Vector2D newVelocity)
      Sets the sprite's velocity to the given Vector2D. The velocity controls how far the sprite moves each time move() is called.
      Parameters:
      newVelocity - the new velocity as a Vector2D

    • setVelocity

      public void setVelocity(float dx, float dy)
      Sets the sprite's velocity using separate x and y values.
      Parameters:
      dx - the new horizontal speed (pixels per frame; positive = right)
      dy - the new vertical speed (pixels per frame; positive = down)

    • move

      public void move()
      Moves the sprite forward one step by adding its velocity to its position, then applies the current screenBehavior to handle screen edges. Call this once per frame inside your draw() loop.

    • getPosition

      public Vector2D getPosition()
      Returns the sprite's current position as a Vector2D.
      Returns:
      the position vector

    • getVelocity

      public Vector2D getVelocity()
      Returns the sprite's current velocity as a Vector2D.
      Returns:
      the velocity vector

    • keyboardControl

      public void keyboardControl()
      Lets the player move this sprite with the arrow keys. Call this inside your sketch's keyPressed() function. The sprite will move at 5 pixels per frame in the pressed direction, and stop when any other key is pressed.

      Example usage: 

         void keyPressed() {
     player.keyboardControl();
   }

    • followMouse

      public void followMouse()
      Moves the sprite toward the mouse cursor at the sprite's current speed. The sprite travels in a straight line toward wherever the mouse is right now. Call this inside draw() to create a "follow the mouse" effect.

      Note: if the sprite's speed is 0, it will not move. Set a velocity first with setVelocity(float, float).

    • distanceTo

      public float distanceTo(Sprite other)
      Returns the straight-line distance in pixels between the center of this sprite and the center of other.
      Parameters:
      other - the sprite to measure to
      Returns:
      the distance in pixels

    • drawSprite

      public abstract void drawSprite()
      Draws this sprite on the screen. Every subclass must provide its own version of this method that contains the actual drawing code (shapes, images, etc.). Call this once per frame inside your draw() loop.
